description: Fix incorrect npm install command in the scaffolded plugin README template. The generated README hardcodes the @opentabs-dev/ scope prefix for the npm install command, but scaffolded plugins use unscoped package names (opentabs-plugin-<name>).

userStories.title: Fix npm install command in scaffolded README to use unscoped package name
userStories.description: The generateReadme function in scaffold.ts produces a README with an npm install command that includes the @opentabs-dev/ scope, but the scaffolded package.json uses the unscoped name opentabs-plugin-<name>. This means the README install instructions will 404 for any community-published plugin.
acceptanceCriteria: The generated README npm install command uses `opentabs-plugin-<name>` without the `@opentabs-dev/` scope prefix
The scaffold.test.ts tests verify the generated README contains the correct unscoped npm install command
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts (fix the npm install line in generateReadme)
- platform/cli/src/scaffold.test.ts (update or add test for README content)

Context:
The `generateReadme` function generates a README.md for scaffolded plugins. The scaffolded package.json name is `opentabs-plugin-${args.name}` (unscoped), but the README contains `npm install -g @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${args.name}` (scoped). Community/user plugins are unscoped — the `@opentabs-dev/` scope is reserved for officially published plugins.

Changes:
In `platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts`, line 300, change:
```
npm install -g @opentabs-dev/opentabs-plugin-${args.name}
```
to:
```
npm install -g opentabs-plugin-${args.name}
```

The surrounding context in the template string (around line 282-331) is the `generateReadme` function. The fix is a single-line change within the template literal.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the `opentabs plugin install ${args.name}` command on line 294 — that command is correct (it resolves both scoped and unscoped names via resolvePluginPackageCandidates).
- Do NOT change the package.json name format (`opentabs-plugin-${args.name}`) — that is correct.
- The docs in `docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx` line 625 already correctly show `npm install -g opentabs-plugin-my-app` — this fix brings the scaffold template in line with the docs.
